This stage sits in startup and early runtime initialization, after configuration and authentication are ready but before the app can confidently serve requests or present full remote-backed UI. Its job is to construct outbound clients and hydrate the external catalogs, settings, and snapshots the rest of the system depends on.

Cloud configuration is the backbone here: cloud-config’s backend adapter decodes bundle responses, the service manages cache/use/retry/validation policy, and bundle_loader exposes that machinery as a startup loader with background refresh. Model availability is prepared in parallel: codex-api and model-provider fetch `/models`, Bedrock contributes a static adapted catalog, and models-manager merges bundled, cached, and remote data into picker-ready presets; utils/oss, ollama, and lmstudio cover OSS-provider readiness and local model discovery/loading. Connector and plugin metadata are also refreshed: connectors normalizes remote directories, chatgpt workspace_settings and connectors determine which connectors/plugins are enabled and visible, rmcp-client provides MCP transport for remote connector access, and core-plugins syncs the curated plugin snapshot locally. Supporting startup checks include backend rate-limit reset access and memories’ startup guard, plus doctor/TUI update probes that refresh release metadata for upgrade UX.
